name: oletools (OLE/Office Document Malware Analysis)

performance.notes: File parsing; pure Python; fast for typical Office documents

agent_readiness.notes: Security analysis tools for OLE/Office documents. CLI tools: olevba (VBA macro extraction), mraptor (macro risk assessment), olebrowse, oleid, oleobj, rtfobj. Python API: from oletools.olevba import VBA_Parser. vba = VBA_Parser('malicious.doc'). for (filename, stream_path, vba_filename, vba_code) in vba.extract_macros(): print(vba_code). vba.analyze_macros() — risk indicators. rtfobj: extract embedded objects from RTF files. oledump: analyze OLE streams. Used by malware analysts, antivirus, email gateways. CIRCL (Computer Incident Response Center Luxembourg).

gotchas: Some obfuscated macros evade detection (not a definitive sandbox)
OOXML (.docx/.xlsx) requires different tools — oletools is for .doc/.xls OLE format
VBA analysis is static — dynamic analysis requires actual execution
ZIP-based Office formats need python-docx or openpyxl for content, not oletools
best_when: Email gateway malware triage for Office attachments
Automated VBA macro extraction and risk assessment
RTF and OLE embedded object analysis
SOC automation for suspicious attachment analysis
avoid_when: OOXML format analysis (.docx/.xlsx) — use python-docx or openpyxl
Dynamic malware analysis (use sandbox like Any.Run, Cuckoo)
summary: oletools analyzes OLE/Office files for malware. VBA_Parser('malicious.doc').extract_macros() extracts VBA code. analyze_macros() returns risk indicators (auto-exec, network access, file write). rtfobj for RTF embedded objects. CLI: olevba, mraptor, oleid. Designed for malware triage and SOC automation. Static analysis only — no dynamic execution. For .docx/.xlsx use python-docx/openpyxl instead. CIRCL/BSD-2.
